rules: Infer from Project purpose and primary Delivery.
Record alternatives and reasons for rejection.
Use ARCH-OTHER only with explicit rationale.
Do not classify Package as an Archetype.

archetypes.name: App Archetype
archetypes.definition: App Archetype applies when the primary Project Delivery is application-like.
selection_indicators: primary Delivery is an application, service, interface, workflow, command, or automation surface
exclusion_indicators: findings or doctrine are the primary Delivery
execution environment itself is the primary Delivery
evaluation_questions: Does the app perform the intended user-facing behavior?
Are interfaces and failure behavior appropriate?
Did package or process concerns displace product value?
archetypes.selection_note: Select from Project purpose and primary Delivery, not from filename keywords alone.

archetypes.name: Research Archetype
archetypes.definition: Research Archetype applies when the main output is knowledge or investigation represented in governed artifacts.
selection_indicators: primary Delivery is findings, analysis, evidence map, corpus, experiment, or research report
exclusion_indicators: a runnable app is the primary Delivery
a profile or prompt artifact is the primary Delivery
evaluation_questions: Are Claims supported by appropriate sources or methods?
Can findings be distinguished from inference?
archetypes.selection_note: Select from Project purpose and primary Delivery, not from filename keywords alone.

archetypes.name: Runtime Archetype
archetypes.definition: Runtime Archetype applies when the deliverable is an environment or capability for executing, building, testing, or orchestrating work.
selection_indicators: primary Delivery is an execution, build, test, sandbox, orchestration, or operational runtime
exclusion_indicators: the main Delivery is an end-user app rather than the environment
the package only defines profile doctrine
evaluation_questions: Can the runtime perform the declared capability?
Are host boundaries and hidden state explicit?
Does proof originate from the delivered runtime?
archetypes.selection_note: Select from Project purpose and primary Delivery, not from filename keywords alone.

archetypes.name: Profile Archetype
archetypes.definition: Profile Archetype applies when the primary output defines meaning, expectations, or operating rules for other work.
selection_indicators: primary Delivery is a glossary, profile, doctrine, policy, schema pack, or governed configuration
exclusion_indicators: prompt behavior is the only primary Delivery
the principal purpose is end-user application behavior
evaluation_questions: Are semantic authorities clear and synchronized?
Are Requirements applicable and not copied blindly?
Do machine guardrails preserve rather than weaken meaning?
archetypes.selection_note: Select from Project purpose and primary Delivery, not from filename keywords alone.

archetypes.name: Prompt Archetype
archetypes.definition: Prompt Archetype applies when the principal deliverable is a Prompt artifact or prompt system.
selection_indicators: primary Delivery is a Prompt, prompt pack, prompt system, or prompt-governed interaction contract
exclusion_indicators: prompts are merely implementation tools for another primary Delivery
evaluation_questions: Does the Prompt preserve Intent and scope?
Do examples bias behavior away from the Goal?
archetypes.selection_note: Select from Project purpose and primary Delivery, not from filename keywords alone.

archetypes.name: Other Archetype
archetypes.definition: Other Archetype is the fallback project-shape classification.
selection_indicators: none of the defined specialized Archetypes adequately describes the primary Delivery
evaluation_questions: Why do specialized Archetypes not apply?
Which custom quality coordinates are required?
What evidence and risks are specific to this Project?
archetypes.selection_note: Select from Project purpose and primary Delivery, not from filename keywords alone.
